items.title: Watch World Cup Day 25: England beats Mexico; Norway eliminates Brazil
items.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

items.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to nearly 3,000
items.description: Eleven days after the devastating twin earthquakes, rescue teams are starting to wrap up searches for survivors while families still try to recover bodies of loved ones from the rubble. Leer más

items.title: Santilli sworn in as Cabinet chief as Milei reshuffles pack
items.description: Diego Santilli formally took over as President Javier Milei's Cabinet chief on Tuesday, completing a transition triggered by Manuel Adorni's resignation amid a corruption investigation. Leer más

items.title: Syria expecting Macron visit in first for Western head-of-state since Assad
items.description: Syria is expecting a visit by French President Emmanuel Macron, the first by a Western European head of state since Syria's new leader Ahmed al-Sharaa took power in 2024. Macron would be accompanied by a delegation "including investors and representatives of French companies". FRANCE 24's Matthew-Mary Caruchet tells us what we know.

items.title: 'Terrifying' roadside assistance ordeal sparks national change
items.description: A woman's campaign for change after her own frightening experience being broken down outside Canberra will see NRMA roadside assistance contractors required to provide a code to customers to verify their identity.

items.title: Maltese tycoon faces trial over Caruana Galizia murder
items.description: The Maltese businessman accused of masterminding journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 murder is in court — seven years after his arrest. Five other men have already been found guilty for their roles in the murder.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Protecting Corsican Scandola Nature Reserve amid tourism boom
items.description: Scandola, a UNESCO-listed nature reserve off the coast of Corsica, is attracting a growing number of visitors from around the world – drawn to its dramatic landscapes and rich marine life. However, protecting its unique wildlife and fragile marine ecosystem is becoming an increasingly delicate balancing act. Our colleagues at France 2 and FRANCE 24's Guillaume Gougeon report.

items.title: Lionel Scaloni reaches century of games with Argentina as AFA eyes new deal
items.description: Argentina's 48-year-old head coach has become the unlikely architect of one of the greatest eras in the nation's sporting history. Leer más

items.title: Quick hits: England star hurt in celebrations, Haaland surprises himself
items.description: England revels in a famous victory over Mexico, but the celebrations come at a cost for one star, while Erling Haaland's brilliance even has the man himself dumbfounded. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

items.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
items.description: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Death toll rises to nearly 3,000
items.description: Rescue operations ⁠to search for ⁠survivors are still underway

items.title: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers
items.description: The Vatican's watchdog authority has imposed severe disciplinary measures on an ultratraditionalist Catholic group. The move comes after the group, the Society of Saint Pius X, consecrated bishops without papal consent.

items.title: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 18 killed in Kyiv region
items.description: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

items.title: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
items.description: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

items.title: Iran: Millions flock to Ali Khamenei's funeral procession
items.description: Crowds have thronged the streets of Tehran in a procession for Iran's late supreme leader. The huge event is seen as a show of strength by the theocratic regime amid peace negotiations with the US.

items.title: Thousands evacuated in southwestern France as wildfire burns out of control
items.description: Ten thousand people in southwestern France were forced to evacuate due to a wildfire that is still burning and has already destroyed 4,600 hectares of forest, authorities said on Monday.

items.title: Border between DR Congo and Rwanda reopens amid Ebola outbreak
items.description: The border between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda has reopened after weeks of closure over the Ebola outbreak. The move restores a vital trade route for the city of Goma, with many residents expressing relief as cross border travel and business resume.

items.title: Venezuelans mobilise to deal with earthquakes' consequences: FRANCE 24 reports
items.description: As more than ten days have passed since Venezuela was struck by twin earthquakes, the death toll remains uncertain, with an official balance of at least 3,342 people killed. On the northern coastal part of the country, the hardest-hit region, survivors and volunteers from across the country are taking matters into their own hands : treating the injured, finding shelter, searching for the missing. FRANCE 24's Pascale Mariani and Maxime Pluvinet spoke to residents

items.title: Marcela Pagano: ‘It’s become evident that it is Karina Milei who calls the shots’
items.description: National deputy says Javier Milei’s government "has covered up Manuel Adorni’s corruption," that voters have fallen out of love with La Libertad Avanza and that Karina Milei, the President’s sister, is the one who’s really in charge in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Nigeria looks to boost security cooperation with Benin and Niger
items.description: Nigeria is working to increase military cooperation with Benin and Niger as jihadists from the Sahel encroach on the country's northwest, the Nigerian defence minister said on Thursday.

items.title: Moldova speeds up its membership process to the EU
items.description: An economically fragile country caught up in Russia’s hybrid war, Moldova is keen to make rapid progress for a quicker membership to the European Union. With the opening of the first chapter of accession negotiations, the EU launched its €1.9 billion investment plan in the country. With this plan, which is conditional on domestic structural reforms, Europe hopes to draw in businesses and drive change in the country.

items.title: Albanians take to the streets in protest for the 35th night in a row
items.description: The nightly demonstrations began over a luxury coastal resort development project in a protected natural area linked to U.S. President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner

items.title: Egypt makes history, heartbreak for Cape Verde at World Cup
items.description: Heartbreak for Cape Verde as the World Cup debutants bowed out after a thrilling 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina. The Blue Sharks twice battled back to level the match, pushing Lionel Messi's side to the limit in one of the tournament's biggest surprise performances.

items.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
items.description: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

items.description: The German Football Associationand national team coach Nagelsmann have parted ways after Germany's poor display at the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Science should be developed away from being 'exclusive club'
items.description: Omar M. Yaghi grew up in the Jordanian capital Ammann in a family of Palestinian refugees. He moved to the United States at the age of 15 and, working his way through academia via Community College, obtained a PhD in chemistry in 1990. He has held professorships at the University of Michigan, UCLA and the University of California at Berkeley. In 2025 he was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry, along with Susumu Kitagawa and Richard Robson, for his work on developing metal–organic frameworks. It was announced this week that Professor Yaghi is to join Tsinghua University in Beijing to lead a new AI-driven research centre.

items.title: ‘We either win or we learn’ – Carlos Queiroz bids emotional farewell to Ghana
items.description: In a heartfelt statement shared on his social media page, the veteran Portuguese tactician expressed pride in what his team accomplished during his tenure, while insisting there is still room for greater ambition and progress The post ‘We either win or we learn’ – Carlos Queiroz bids emotional farewell to Ghana appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .
